title: Share of artificial intelligence jobs among all job postings
subtitle: A job posting is considered an AI job if it requests one or more AI skills, e.g., "natural language processing", "neural networks", "machine learning", or "robotics".
caption:
Share of artificial intelligence jobs among all job postings
A job posting is considered an AI job if it requests one or more AI skills, e.g., "natural language processing", "neural networks", "machine learning", or "robotics".

Across nine countries between 2014 and 2024, the share of job postings requiring artificial intelligence skills varies by nation and generally trends upward over time. The vertical axis measures this share as a percentage ranging from 0% to 1.8%. The United States consistently occupies the highest position throughout the timeline, starting near 0.6% in 2014 and rising to reach 1.8% by 2024. The United Kingdom begins around 0.4% in 2014 and climbs to approximately 1.3% by the end of the period. Sweden, the Netherlands, and Spain all show data beginning in 2019 or 2020, with Sweden reaching roughly 1.3% in 2024, the Netherlands hitting 1.25%, and Spain ending near 1.2%. Austria’s line starts at 0.6% in 2019 and finishes just above 1.0% in 2024. Italy’s trajectory begins at 0.5% in 2019 and ends near 0.9% in 2024. New Zealand remains at the bottom of the chart across all years, starting below 0.2% in 2014 and rising to about 0.55% by 2024. Most lines show growth over time, though some exhibit dips — for example, the United States drops from 1.7% in 2022 to 1.4% in 2023 before rebounding. The United Kingdom also dips slightly between 2022 and 2023. The data shows that while AI job postings remain a small fraction of total postings everywhere, their prevalence has increased substantially in most countries over the past decade, with the United States leading in absolute share and growth magnitude.
